description:
Rules are evaluated from top to bottom, and the first matching rule applies. Rule conditions are evaluated before Service Id Contributors are applied. Note that conditions do not modify attributes of requests. If conditions match, then Service Id Contributors are applied. **All of the Contributors are always applied.** But it is possible to influence the creation of Services by choosing how they get transformed.\
More extensive information on Service detection rules can be found [here](https://dt-url.net/9i03b79).
displayName: Service detection rules for Full Web Services

contributionType.description: Defines whether the original value should be used or transformations should be applied.

items.description: Use the value as it is.
Apply transformations to the original value.
Extract segments from the URL path and optionally transform them.

contributionTypeWithOverride.description: Defines whether the original value should be used, transformations should be applied or it should be overridden to a constant value.
contributionTypeWithOverride.displayName: Contribution type with the ability to override to constant value

items.description: Use the value as it is.
Apply transformations to the original value.

conditions.description: A list of conditions necessary for the rule to take effect. If multiple conditions are specified, they must **all** match a Request for the rule to apply. If there is no condition at all, the rule is always applied. Conditions are evaluated against attributes, but do not modify them.
conditions.documentation: Each list item compares one detected attribute with one compare operation. Multiple condition entries are combined with **AND**.

description.description: A short description of the rule.

enabled.description: If disabled, the rule is skipped during evaluation.

idContributors.description: Contributors to the Service Identifier calculation. All of the Contributors are always applied.

managementZones.description: Define a management zone of the process group for which this service detection rule should be created.

name.description: The name of the rule. It is used for identification and has no effect on the rule logic.

condition.description: Matches requests by comparing one detected attribute with one operation.

attribute.description: The detected attribute that should be compared with the specified operation.
attribute.displayName: Take the value of this attribute

compareOperationType.description: The type of comparison operation that should be applied to the detected attribute.
compareOperationType.documentation:
When using this field over the Settings API, it is stored as a string and must use one of the fixed compare-operation identifiers. The available subset depends on the selected `attribute`.

- `Exists`, `NotExists`
- `BoolIsTrue`, `BoolIsFalse`
- `TagEquals`, `TagKeyEquals`
- `StringEquals`, `NotStringEquals`, `StringStartsWith`, `NotStringStartsWith`, `StringEndsWith`, `NotStringEndsWith`, `StringContains`, `NotStringContains`
- `FrameworkEquals`, `NotFrameworkEquals`
- `IpInRange`, `NotIpInRange`
- `IntEquals`, `NotIntEquals`, `IntGreaterThan`, `IntLessThan`

framework.description:
The technology that should be compared with the detected attribute.

Select one or more technologies. The condition matches if the detected attribute value equals (for `FrameworkEquals`) or does not equal (for `NotFrameworkEquals`) at least one of the selected technologies.

intValue.description: The integer value to compare the detected attribute with.

intValues.description: If multiple values are specified, at least one of them must match for the condition to match.

ipRangeFrom.description: The beginning of the IP range. The condition matches if the detected attribute value is greater than or equal to this value (for `IpInRange`) or less than this value (for `NotIpInRange`).

ipRangeTo.description: The end of the IP range. The condition matches if the detected attribute value is less than or equal to this value (for `IpInRange`) or greater than this value (for `NotIpInRange`).

tagValues.description: If multiple values are specified, at least one of them must match for the condition to match.

textValues.description: If multiple values are specified, at least one of them must match for the condition to match

condition.summaryPattern: {attribute|enumValue, select, IpInRange {{attribute} {compareOperationType|toLowerCase} from {ipRangeFrom} to {ipRangeTo}} NotIpInRange {{attribute} {compareOperationType|toLowerCase} from {ipRangeFrom} to {ipRangeTo}} other {{attribute} {compareOperationType|toLowerCase} {textValues}{tagValues}{intValue}{framework}}}

contextIdContributor.description: Enables and configures the context root as a Service Id contributor.
contextIdContributor.documentation: Set `enableIdContributor` to `true` to let the context root contribute to the Service Id and configure the nested `serviceIdContributor` block. If the switch stays `false`, this contributor does not participate in service detection.

enableIdContributor.description: When enabled, the context root contributes to the Service Id.
enableIdContributor.displayName: Transform this value before letting it contribute to the Service Id

serviceIdContributor.description: Choose how to transform the context root value before it contributes to the Service Id. Note that all of the Transformations are always applied. Transformations are applied in the order they are specified, and the output of the previous transformation is the input for the next one.

contextRoot.description: Configures how the detected URL context root contributes to the Service Id.

contributionType.description: Defines whether the original value should be used or if a transformation set should be used to override a value or transform it.

segmentCount.description: The number of segments of the URL to be kept. The URL is divided by slashes (/), the indexing starts with 1 at context root. For example, if you specify 2 for the `www.dynatrace.com/support/help/dynatrace-api/` URL, the value of `support/help` is used.
segmentCount.displayName: Segments to copy from URL path

transformations.description: Choose how to transform a value before it contributes to the Service Id. Note that all of the Transformations are always applied. Transformations are applied in the order they are specified, and the output of the previous transformation is the input for the next one. The resulting value contributes to the Service Id and can be found on the **Service overview page** under **Properties and tags**.

valueOverride.description: The value to be used instead of the detected value.

constraints.customMessage: At least one service identifier contributor (application identifier, URL context root, server name, web service name or web service namespace) must exist.

idContributorsType.description: Defines which detected values contribute to the Service Id for matching full web services.

applicationId.description: Contribute to the Service Id calculation from the detected application identifier.
applicationId.documentation: You can keep the detected value, override it with a constant value, or apply transformations before it contributes to the Service Id.

contextRoot.description: The context root is the first segment of the request URL after the Server name. For example, in the `www.dynatrace.com/support/help/dynatrace-api/` URL the context root is `/support`. The context root value can be found on the **Service overview page** under **Properties and tags**.
contextRoot.documentation: You can keep the detected context root, replace it with a constant value, copy a configurable number of URL path segments, or apply context-root transformations. If URL segment copying and transformations are both configured, transformations run on the copied value.

detectAsWebRequestService.description:
Detect the matching requests as full web services (false) or web request services (true).

Setting this field to true prevents detecting of matching requests as full web services. A web request service is created instead. If you need to further modify the resulting web request service, you need to create a separate [Full web request rule](builtin:service-detection.full-web-request).
detectAsWebRequestService.documentation: When this option is enabled, the contributor settings below are ignored because matching requests are detected as full web request services instead of full web services.

serverName.description: Contribute to the Service Id calculation from the detected server name.

webServiceName.description: Contribute to the Service Id calculation from the detected web service name.

webServiceNamespace.description: Contribute to the Service Id calculation from the detected web service namespace.

reducedTransformation.description: Transforms a detected context root value before it contributes to the Service Id.

includeHexNumbers.description: Whether to also remove hexadecimal numbers (sequences of at least `minDigitCount` hexadecimal digits preceded by '0x'). It is used only when the transformation type is `REMOVE_NUMBERS`.

minDigitCount.description: The minimum number of digits that a numeric sequence must have to be removed. It is used only when the transformation type is `REMOVE_NUMBERS`.

prefix.description: The part of the text that serves as a reference point for the transformation. Its use depends on the transformation type.

replacementValue.description: The text that replaces the part between `prefix` and `suffix`. It is used only when the transformation type is `REPLACE_BETWEEN`.

suffix.description: The part of the text that serves as a reference point for the transformation. Its use depends on the transformation type.

transformationType.description: Defines what kind of transformation will be applied on the original value.

reducedTransformation.summaryPattern: {transformationType|enumValue, select, BEFORE {Use value before {suffix}} REPLACE_BETWEEN {Replace value between {prefix|valueSet, select, true {{prefix}} other {the beginning}} and {suffix|valueSet, select, true {{suffix}} other {the end}} with {replacementValue}} REMOVE_NUMBERS {Remove numbers {minDigitCount|valueSet, select, true {with a minimum of {minDigitCount, plural, one {# digit} other {# digits}}} other {}}} REMOVE_CREDIT_CARDS {Remove credit card numbers} REMOVE_IBANS {Remove IBANs} REMOVE_IPS {Remove IPs} other {}}

serviceIdContributor.description: Enables and configures a Service Id contributor.
serviceIdContributor.documentation: Set `enableIdContributor` to `true` to let the detected value contribute to the Service Id and configure the nested `serviceIdContributor` block. If the switch stays `false`, this contributor does not participate in service detection.

enableIdContributor.description: When enabled, the detected value contributes to the Service Id.
enableIdContributor.displayName: Transform this value before letting it contribute to the Service Id

serviceIdContributor.description: Choose how to transform the detected value before it contributes to the Service Id. Note that all of the Transformations are always applied. Transformations are applied in the order they are specified, and the output of the previous transformation is the input for the next one.

transformation.description: Transforms a detected contributor value before it contributes to the Service Id.

includeHexNumbers.description: Whether to also remove hexadecimal numbers (sequences of at least `minDigitCount` hexadecimal digits preceded by '0x'). It is used only when the transformation type is `REMOVE_NUMBERS`.

minDigitCount.description: The minimum number of digits that a numeric sequence must have to be removed. It is used only when the transformation type is `REMOVE_NUMBERS`.

prefix.description: The part of the text that serves as a reference point for the transformation. Its use depends on the transformation type.

replacementValue.description: The text that replaces the part between `prefix` and `suffix`. It is used only when the transformation type is `REPLACE_BETWEEN`.

segmentCount.description: How many segments should be taken.

selectIndex.description: The index of the element to keep after splitting. The index is zero-based. It is used only when the transformation type is `SPLIT_SELECT`.

splitDelimiter.description: The delimiter used for splitting the text. It is used only when the transformation type is `SPLIT_SELECT` or `TAKE_SEGMENTS`.

suffix.description: The part of the text that serves as a reference point for the transformation. Its use depends on the transformation type.

takeFromEnd.description: Whether to take segments from the end of the text instead of the beginning. It is used only when the transformation type is `TAKE_SEGMENTS`.

transformationType.description: Defines what kind of transformation will be applied on the original value.

transformation.summaryPattern: {transformationType|enumValue, select, BEFORE {Use value before {suffix}} AFTER {Use value after {prefix}} BETWEEN {Use value between {prefix|valueSet, select, true {{prefix}} other {the beginning}} and {suffix|valueSet, select, true {{suffix}} other {the end}}} REPLACE_BETWEEN {Replace value between {prefix|valueSet, select, true {{prefix}} other {the beginning}} and {suffix|valueSet, select, true {{suffix}} other {the end}} with {replacementValue}} REMOVE_NUMBERS {Remove numbers {minDigitCount|valueSet, select, true {with a minimum of {minDigitCount, plural, one {# digit} other {# digits}}} other {}}} REMOVE_CREDIT_CARDS {Remove credit card numbers} REMOVE_IBANS {Remove IBANs} REMOVE_IPS {Remove IPs} SPLIT_SELECT {Split text by {splitDelimiter} and select the element at index {selectIndex}} TAKE_SEGMENTS {Split text by {splitDelimiter} and take the {takeFromEnd, select, true {last} other {first}} {segmentCount, plural, one {# element} other {# elements}}} other {}}

transformationSet.description: Configures how a detected contributor value contributes to the Service Id.

contributionType.description: Defines whether the original value should be used or if a transformation set should be used to override a value or transform it.

transformations.description: Choose how to transform a value before it contributes to the Service Id. Note that all of the Transformations are always applied. Transformations are applied in the order they are specified, and the output of the previous transformation is the input for the next one. The resulting value contributes to the Service Id and can be found on the **Service overview page** under **Properties and tags**.

valueOverride.description: The value to be used instead of the detected value.
Defines the constant value used when overriding a detected contributor value.
valueOverride.documentation: Provide the fixed value that should replace the detected one when `contributionType` is `OverrideValue`.

value.description: The value to be used instead of the detected value.
